themes: model competition (Opus 4.7 vs GPT-5.5, Mythos holdback)

worthClicking.author: @ClaudeDevs
worthClicking.why: Official Claude Code quality regression post-mortem: three root causes found, all fixed in v2.1.116+, usage limits reset — essential reading for anyone relying on Claude Code in production.

worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: Greg Brockman's GPT-5.5 positioning statement explicitly frames it as a new compute-work paradigm beyond coding — signals how OpenAI is selling this to enterprise.

worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: Codex + 5.5 pitched as general computer-use for spreadsheets and slides, not just code — direct relevance to sales enablement and knowledge worker displacement.

worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: Brockman personally offering Codex enterprise rollouts via email — rare direct signal of where OpenAI is pushing enterprise distribution right now.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Sharp take on how Claude's new third-party integrations (Tripadvisor, Instacart, TurboTax, etc.) threaten the demand-aggregation moat of every major marketplace platform.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Concrete pricing trajectory analysis — GPT-5 to 5.5 is an 8x input token price increase in 8 months — useful framing for AI product cost modeling.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Ethan Mollick's hands-on GPT-5.5 Pro verdict after weeks of testing: best model for hard problems today, with real autonomous research results — most credible third-party eval in the feed.

worthClicking.author: @ClaudeDevs
worthClicking.why: Claude Managed Agents persistent memory is now in public beta — agents can learn across sessions, memory is file-based and API-exportable, directly relevant to building stateful AI products.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Clean conceptual reframe: the per-app model was a pre-LLM constraint on information architecture, and AI collapses the marginal cost of adding new domains — useful product strategy lens.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: Box CEO reports a real 10-point accuracy jump on internal enterprise content evals for GPT-5.5 vs 5.4 — one of the few concrete enterprise benchmark disclosures in the feed.

voices.why: Primary source for Anthropic platform changes — post-mortem and memory beta both broke here first.
Consistently sharp on model competition dynamics, pricing strategy, and PM role evolution — several posts directly on-target for all three operator interests.
Most credible hands-on GPT-5.5 evaluator in the feed; cuts through hype with actual use-case results.
Thinking clearly about the app-paradigm shift and intent-driven interfaces — good product strategy signal, low noise ratio.
Greg Brockman actively selling Codex enterprise and framing GPT-5.5 positioning — worth watching for OpenAI go-to-market signals.

examples: RoundtableSpace SHOUTING tool roundups with no analysis
off-topic lifestyle content (morning routines, MJ biopic, eye parasites)
